# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id

  AND i.available_today > 0;
SELECT DISTINCT kind_id FROM products ORDER BY kind_id LIMIT 200;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'automotive_cleaners'
  AND s.city = 'Salzburg'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;
SELECT id, name FROM stores WHERE name LIKE '%Graz%' AND name LIKE '%Lend%';

request.stdin:
WITH target AS (
  SELECT 'item1' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.name='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Ajax Eco Classic%'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'item2' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated%'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')='8'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'item3' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated TopFix%'
    AND p.model='GTU-YPJ'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')='3'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'item4' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated%'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='threaded rod'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'item5' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Festool'
    AND p.name='Tool Box and Bag'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Festool Stackable%'
    AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='parts case'
)
SELECT t.item, t.sku, t.path, i.available_today
FROM target t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
ORDER BY t.item;

request.stdin:
WITH targets AS (
  SELECT 't1' as t, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.category='Sealant'
    AND p.brand='Sika'
    AND p.series='Sika Professional'
    AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.sealant_type')='silicone sealant'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't2', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.category='Corded Angle Grinder'
    AND p.brand='Bosch'
    AND p.series='Bosch Compact'
    AND p.model='PKS 375-EOK'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.disc_diameter')='150 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't3', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.category='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND p.brand='Dresselhaus'
    AND p.series='Dresselhaus Outdoor Pro'
    AND p.model='1VG-P5G'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='5 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't4', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.category='Cordless Drill Driver'
    AND p.brand='Metabo'
    AND p.series='Metabo Brushless'
    AND p.model='BS 1OW-IK5'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='18 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.battery_platform')='18v-system'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.kit_contents')='case'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't5', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.category='Cordless Drill Driver'
    AND p.brand='Metabo'
    AND p.series='Metabo Brushless'
    AND p.model='BS 1OW-IK5'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='12 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.battery_platform')='12v-system'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.kit_contents')='case'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't6', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.category='Pipe Fitting'
    AND p.brand='Viega'
    AND p.series='Viega Professional'
    AND p.model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fitting_type')='pipe fitting'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='15 mm'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connection_type')='push fit'
)
SELECT t, sku, path, available_today, CASE WHEN available_today>=3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies
FROM targets
ORDER BY t;

name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

WITH targets AS (
SELECT sku,path,'uvex' AS tag FROM products
WHERE brand='Uvex' AND series='Bionic' AND model='x-fit Y59-F8N'
  AND kind_id='work_jackets'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='yellow'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.size')='M'
UNION ALL
SELECT sku,path,'caparol' FROM products
WHERE brand='Caparol' AND series='Premium' AND model='Capa 32D-5X8'
  AND kind_id='metal_concrete_paint'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.product_type')='primer'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='red'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.finish')='satin'
UNION ALL
SELECT sku,path,'fischer' FROM products
WHERE brand='Fischer' AND series='Universal' AND model='SX 1PF-JY8'
  AND kind_id='wood_drywall_screws'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm')=5
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.length_mm')=80
UNION ALL
SELECT sku,path,'bostik' FROM products
WHERE brand='Bostik' AND model='3S9-FF7'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.sealant_type')='sanitary sealant'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='gray'
UNION ALL
SELECT sku,path,'ajax' FROM products
WHERE brand='Ajax' AND model='252-QRO'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.volume_ml')=250
UNION ALL
SELECT sku,path,'bosch' FROM products
WHERE brand='Bosch' AND model='PKS 3Q6-CLK'
  AND kind_id='workshop_saws_cutters'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.machine_type')='scroll saw'
)
SELECT t.tag,t.sku,t.path,i.available_today
FROM targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id='store_vienna_meidling'
ORDER BY t.tag;
SELECT id, name FROM stores WHERE name LIKE '%Graz%' OR name LIKE '%Jakomini%';

WITH targets AS (
  SELECT 't1' as t, p.sku, p.path, p.category_id, p.kind_id, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.brand='Sika' AND p.series='Sika Professional' AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.sealant_type')='silicone sealant'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't2', p.sku, p.path, p.category_id, p.kind_id, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.brand='Bosch' AND p.series='Bosch Compact' AND p.model='PKS 375-EOK'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.disc_diameter')='150 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't3', p.sku, p.path, p.category_id, p.kind_id, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.brand='Dresselhaus' AND p.series='Dresselhaus Outdoor Pro' AND p.model='1VG-P5G'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='5 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't4', p.sku, p.path, p.category_id, p.kind_id, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  WHERE i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
    AND p.brand='Metabo' AND p.series='Metabo Brushless' AND p.model='BS 1OW-IK5'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='18 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.battery_platform')='18v-system'

    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='15 mm'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connection_type')='push fit'
)
SELECT * FROM targets ORDER BY t;

request.stdin:
SELECT brand, series, model, sku, path, kind_id, category_id, properties
FROM products
WHERE (brand='Sika' AND series LIKE '%Sika Professional%' AND model LIKE '%28T%UV8%')
   OR (brand='Bosch' AND series LIKE '%Bosch Compact%' AND model LIKE '%PKS 375-EOK%')
   OR (brand='Dresselhaus' AND series LIKE '%Dresselhaus Outdoor Pro%' AND model LIKE '%1VG-P5G%')
   OR (brand='Metabo' AND series LIKE '%Metabo Brushless%' AND model LIKE '%BS 1OW-IK5%')
   OR (brand='Viega' AND series LIKE '%Viega Professional%' AND model LIKE '%Sanpress 2OG-ENN%')
LIMIT 50;

WITH target_products AS (
  SELECT 'p1' AS req, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category_id='wall_paint' AND brand='Jub' AND series LIKE '%Jub Jupol Akrilin%' AND model='296-K0S'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Yellow'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.finish')='matte'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p2' AS req, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category_id='cleaning_machines' AND brand='Karcher' AND series LIKE '%Karcher Workshop%' AND model='T2D-J9K'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.machine_type')='steam cleaner'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.power')='1400 W'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p3' AS req, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category_id='storage_bin_organizer' AND brand='Raaco' AND series LIKE '%Raaco Clear Lid CarryLite%' AND model='2PF-6FB'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='storage bin'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Clear'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p4' AS req, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category_id='pliers_wrenches' AND brand='Bahco' AND series LIKE '%Bahco Comfort Grip S%' AND model='RQL-3LS'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='side cutter'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.length')='180 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p5' AS req, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category_id='respiratory_protection' AND brand='Portwest' AND series LIKE '%Portwest Comfort KX%' AND model='XLD-KFS'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.mask_type')='half mask'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p6' AS req, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category_id='wall_paint' AND brand='Tikkurila' AND series LIKE '%Tikkurila Interior TIK%' AND model='M0N-N0K'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Gray'
)
SELECT tp.req, tp.sku, tp.path, i.available_today
FROM target_products tp
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = tp.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_jakomini'
ORDER BY tp.req;

SELECT brand, series, model, sku, path, kind_id, category_id, name
FROM products
WHERE brand IN ('Sika','Bosch','Dresselhaus','Metabo','Viega')
ORDER BY brand, series, model
LIMIT 300;

WITH target_skus AS (
  SELECT 'WRK-3IQBT0IW' AS sku UNION ALL
  SELECT 'PNT-2OM7CJKJ' UNION ALL
  SELECT 'FST-145D38AS' UNION ALL
  SELECT 'ADH-1WLPXSH6' UNION ALL
  SELECT 'CLN-1JX4EDE7' UNION ALL
  SELECT 'MAC-1CPCA5N3'
)
SELECT t.sku, p.path, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM target_skus t
JOIN products p ON p.sku=t.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id='store_vienna_meidling'
ORDER BY t.sku;

SELECT brand, series, model, sku, path, category_id, properties
FROM products
WHERE (brand='Jub' AND model='296-K0S')
   OR (brand='Karcher' AND model='T2D-J9K')
   OR (brand='Raaco' AND model='2PF-6FB')
   OR (brand='Bahco' AND model='RQL-3LS')
   OR (brand='Portwest' AND model='XLD-KFS')
   OR (brand='Tikkurila' AND model='M0N-N0K')
ORDER BY brand, model
LIMIT 20;
SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
basket_lines,"CREATE TABLE basket_lines (
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (basket_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
baskets,"CREATE TABLE baskets (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	discount_percent INTEGER,
	discount_reason_code TEXT,
	discount_issuer_id TEXT,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
categories,"CREATE TABLE categories (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	department TEXT NOT NULL
)"
customers,"CREATE TABLE customers (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	home_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	home_lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
employee_roles,"CREATE TABLE employee_roles (
	employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	role TEXT NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (employee_id, role),
	FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employees(id)
)"
employees,"CREATE TABLE employees (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	title TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
families,"CREATE TABLE families (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payment_lines,"CREATE TABLE payment_lines (
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	unit_price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (payment_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payments,"CREATE TABLE payments (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_archived INTEGER NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_method_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	device_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	observed_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	observed_lon REAL NOT NULL,
	three_ds_status TEXT,
	three_ds_failure_reason TEXT,
	three_ds_attempts INTEGER,
	three_ds_max_attempts INTEGER,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
product_kinds,"CREATE TABLE product_kinds (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL
)"
product_properties,"CREATE TABLE product_properties (
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	key TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_text TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_number REAL,
	PRIMARY KEY (sku, key)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
returns,"CREATE TABLE returns (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	reason_code TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id)
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"

  JOIN product_properties b ON b.sku=p.sku AND b.key='power_w' AND b.value_text='1400'
  WHERE p.brand='Karcher' AND p.series='Workshop' AND p.model='K T2D-J9K'
),
p3 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_properties a ON a.sku=p.sku AND a.key='storage_type' AND a.value_text='storage bin'
  JOIN product_properties b ON b.sku=p.sku AND b.key='color_family' AND b.value_text='clear'
  WHERE p.brand='Raaco' AND p.series='Clear Lid' AND p.model='CarryLite 2PF-6FB'
),
p4 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_properties a ON a.sku=p.sku AND a.key='tool_type' AND a.value_text='side cutter'
  JOIN product_properties b ON b.sku=p.sku AND b.key='length_mm' AND b.value_text='180'
  WHERE p.brand='Bahco' AND p.series='Comfort Grip' AND p.model='S RQL-3LS'
),
p5 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_properties a ON a.sku=p.sku AND a.key='mask_type' AND a.value_text='half mask'
  WHERE p.brand='Portwest' AND p.series='Comfort' AND p.model='KX XLD-KFS'
),
p6 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_properties a ON a.sku=p.sku AND a.key='color_family' AND a.value_text='gray'
  WHERE p.brand='Tikkurila' AND p.series='Interior' AND p.model='TIK M0N-N0K'
),
req AS (
  SELECT 'p1' req, sku, path FROM p1
  UNION ALL SELECT 'p2', sku, path FROM p2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'p3', sku, path FROM p3
  UNION ALL SELECT 'p4', sku, path FROM p4
  UNION ALL SELECT 'p5', sku, path FROM p5
  UNION ALL SELECT 'p6', sku, path FROM p6
)
SELECT r.req, r.sku, r.path, i.available_today
FROM req r
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=r.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_jakomini'
ORDER BY r.req;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.store_id, i.available_today
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
WHERE p.brand = 'Knipex'
  AND p.category_id = 'pliers_wrenches'
  AND p.series = 'Knipex Professional'
  AND p.model = 'KP 2H0-CV1'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.tool_type') = 'combination pliers'
  AND i.store_id IN ('store_graz_jakomini','store_graz_lend')
ORDER BY i.store_id;

SELECT s.id, s.path, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM stores s
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id = s.id AND i.sku = 'WRK-3JV3G2YY'
WHERE s.city = 'Vienna'
ORDER BY s.id;

SELECT sku, path, brand, category_id, series, model, json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type') AS tool_type
FROM products
WHERE brand = 'Knipex'
  AND (model = 'KP 2H0-CV1' OR series LIKE '%Knipex Professional%')
LIMIT 50;

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.
